expectations.text: A PRD file exists in outputs
expectations.evidence: File PRD-002-notification-system.md exists in the outputs directory.

expectations.text: PRD describes Firestore collection or schema for notifications
expectations.evidence: Lines 129-147: '### Data Model Changes' section describes a new Firestore collection 'notifications' with a full TypeScript interface including fields: id, userId, type, processId, processTitle, oldStatus, newStatus, triggeredBy, triggeredByName, read, createdAt. Also specifies Firestore indexes needed.

expectations.text: Email notifications are scoped to Phase 2 or later, not Phase 1
expectations.evidence: Lines 208-224: Email notifications are explicitly in 'Phase 3: Email Notifications'. Phase 1 (lines 164-186) is 'Client-Side In-App Notifications' only. FR-11 on line 69 marks email as 'Could (Phase 3)'.

Lines 90-101: '## Acceptance Criteria' section with 12 checkbox items covering notification creation, bell icon, dropdown, navigation, mark-as-read, real-time updates, and mobile responsiveness. Additional phase-level acceptance criteria at lines 181-186, 203-206, 220-224, and 236-239.

expectations.text: A ROADMAP.md file exists in outputs
expectations.evidence: File ROADMAP.md exists in the outputs directory.

expectations.text: Discovery or clarifying questions were asked before writing the PRD
expectations.evidence: Lines 259-263: The PRD contains an 'Open Questions' section with 3 questions for the user (badge persistence across sessions, admin email list source, email sender domain). These indicate a discovery process, though no separate transcript shows questions asked before writing began.
